no_test_on_users.principle: Code must work immediately, no testing on real users
no_test_on_users.rationale: Every code deployment affects real users, must be production-ready from first run
no_test_on_users.implementation: Pre-execution validation, self-diagnostics, and immediate functionality

fix_and_advance.principle: When wrong, fix immediately; when fixing, must progress
fix_and_advance.behavior_pattern: Acknowledge error → Fix → Ensure improvement → Document lesson
fix_and_advance.key_metrics: Each fix should make system better than before, not just restore status quo

no_dependency_attitude.principle: No reliance on others - take full responsibility
no_dependency_attitude.manifestation: Own the code, own the errors, own the solutions
no_dependency_attitude.cultural_impact: Creates self-sufficient engineers who drive systems forward

fix_progression_rules: Fix must include preventative measures for same error
System better after fix than before error
Lesson integrated into team knowledge base
responsibility_framework: Single point of accountability for each component

effective: Ownership of both problem and solution

team_norms: Solution ownership from discovery to deployment

engineering_culture: Code once, deploy immediately - no 'test environments'
Every error fixed must advance the system
Full ownership means full responsibility - no excuses
User value delivery is the only validation metric
technical_standards: All scripts must pass pre-execution self-validation
Mobile-first is non-negotiable for UI development
Rate-limiting built into all batch operations
Permission management follows least privilege principle
operational_excellence: Quota awareness integrated into all designs
Automation requires stricter validation than manual work
